concerns.text: Task T025 defines straight-lining detection as 'Calculate variance of the multi-item scale scores (one score per post) across ALL 5 posts'. This contradicts the spec (FR-003, Edge Case 2) which defines straight-lining as 'zero variance across 7 items' (the scale items themselves). The task description is ambiguous and implements the wrong logic (variance across posts vs. variance across scale items), making it impossible to execute correctly without guessing the intended metric.

Task T051 mandates a 'hard pipeline failure' if the stratification difference exceeds a threshold (e.g., 0.05). The spec (SC-006, Edge Cases) only requires 'reporting' the difference to verify validity; it does not authorize halting execution. An implementer cannot execute this task deterministically because the spec does not define the threshold value (0.05 is arbitrary in the task) nor the failure behavior, creating a conflict between task instruction and spec requirement.

Tasks T041 and T042 require raising an error if memory/runtime thresholds are exceeded. The spec (FR-007, SC-005) only requires the system to 'ensure' operations complete within constraints (a performance goal), not to implement a runtime abort mechanism. The tasks lack a defined threshold value (e.g., '7 GB' is in the spec, but the task says 'predefined threshold' without defining it in the task description), making execution non-deterministic.

Task T009b says 'Document that the calculated N matches... in code/config.py as a comment'. This is too coarse and vague. It does not specify the exact file path, the exact comment format, or the verification step (e.g., 'grep for comment'). The downstream atomizer will need to split this into 'Run calculation', 'Write specific comment string', and 'Verify comment exists'.

Task T037 requires the report to include 'Explicit justification text for the model choice'. It does not specify the exact text or the logic for generating it (e.g., 'If ICC >= 0.05, write "LME used due to ICC=..."'). The implementer must guess the phrasing, violating the 'concrete deliverable' rule.
